classes.name: EnableAmendment
classes.signature: class xrpl.models.transactions.pseudo_transactions.EnableAmendment(*, account_txn_id: Optional[str] = None, flags: int | ~typing.List[int] = 0, last_ledger_sequence: Optional[int] = None, memos: Optional[List[Memo]] = None, signers: Optional[List[Signer]] = None, ticket_sequence: Optional[int] = None, network_id: Optional[int] = None, amendment: str = <object object>, ledger_sequence: int = <object object>, tf_got_majority: bool | None = None, tf_lost_majority: bool | None = None)
classes.description:
Bases: PseudoTransaction
An EnableAmendment pseudo-transaction marks a change in status of an amendment to the XRP Ledger protocol, including:
A proposed amendment gained supermajority approval from validators.
A proposed amendment lost supermajority approval.
A proposed amendment has been enabled.
The address of the sender of the transaction. Required.
A hash value identifying a previous transaction from the same sender. If provided, this transaction is only considered valid if the identified transaction is the most recent transaction sent by this address. See AccountTxnID for details.
A unique identifier for the amendment. This is not intended to be a human-readable name. See Amendments for a list of known amendments. This field is required.
Creates the canonical binary format of the Transaction object.
The binary-encoded object, as a hexadecimal string.
(Auto-fillable) The amount of XRP to destroy as a cost to send this transaction. See Transaction Cost for details.
The Flags value of the EnableAmendment pseudo-transaction indicates the status of the amendment at the time of the ledger including the pseudo-transaction. A Flags value of 0 (no flags) or an omitted Flags field indicates that the amendment has been enabled, and applies to all ledgers afterward.
Decodes a transaction blob.
tx_blob – the tx blob to decode.
The formatted transaction.
Construct a new Transaction from a dictionary of parameters.
value – The value to construct the Transaction from.
A new Transaction object, constructed using the given parameters.
XRPLModelException – If the dictionary provided is invalid.
Creates a Transaction object based on a JSON or JSON-string representation of data
In Payment transactions, the DeliverMax field is renamed to the Amount field.
value – The dictionary or JSON string to be instantiated.
A Transaction object instantiated from the input.
XRPLModelException – If Payment transactions have different values for amount and deliver_max fields
Hashes the Transaction object as the ledger does. Only valid for signed Transaction objects.
The hash of the Transaction object.
XRPLModelException – if the Transaction is unsigned.
Returns the correct transaction type based on the string name.
transaction_type – The String name of the Transaction object.
The transaction class with the given name.
XRPLModelException – If transaction_type is not a valid Transaction type.
Returns whether the transaction has the given flag value set.
flag – The given flag value for which the function will determine whether it is set.
Whether the transaction has the given flag value set.
Checks whether the provided dictionary is a dictionary representation of this class.
Note: This only checks the exact model, and does not count model inheritance. This method returns False if the dictionary represents a subclass of this class.
dictionary – The dictionary to check. Note: The input dictionary can be of non-dict type. For instance, a str representation of JSON.
True if dictionary is a dict representation of an instance of this class; False if not.
Checks if a transaction has been signed.
Whether the transaction has been signed
Returns whether this BaseModel is valid.
Whether this BaseModel is valid.
The highest ledger index this transaction can appear in. Specifying this field places a strict upper limit on how long the transaction can wait to be validated or rejected. See Reliable Transaction Submission for details.
The ledger index where this pseudo-transaction appears. This distinguishes the pseudo-transaction from other occurrences of the same change. This field is required.
Additional arbitrary information attached to this transaction.
The network id of the transaction.
(Auto-fillable) The sequence number of the transaction. Must match the sending account’s next unused sequence number. See Account Sequence for details.
Signing data authorizing a multi-signed transaction. Added during multi-signing.
The public key authorizing a single-signed transaction. Automatically added during signing.
An arbitrary source tag representing a hosted user or specific purpose at the sending account where this transaction comes from.
Support for this amendment increased to at least 80% of trusted validators starting with this ledger version.
Support for this amendment decreased to less than 80% of trusted validators starting with this ledger version.
The sequence number of the ticket to use in place of a Sequence number. If this is provided, sequence must be 0. Cannot be used with account_txn_id.
Returns the dictionary representation of a Transaction.
The dictionary representation of a Transaction.
Creates a JSON-like dictionary in the JSON format used by the binary codec based on the Transaction object.
A JSON-like dictionary in the JSON format used by the binary codec.
The cryptographic signature from the sender that authorizes this transaction. Automatically added during signing.
Raises if this object is invalid.
XRPLModelException – if this object is invalid.

attributes.name: account: str
attributes.description: The address of the sender of the transaction. Required.

attributes.name: account_txn_id: str | None = None
attributes.description: A hash value identifying a previous transaction from the same sender. If provided, this transaction is only considered valid if the identified transaction is the most recent transaction sent by this address. See AccountTxnID for details.

attributes.name: amendment: str
attributes.description: A unique identifier for the amendment. This is not intended to be a human-readable name. See Amendments for a list of known amendments. This field is required.

attributes.name: fee: str = '0'
attributes.description: (Auto-fillable) The amount of XRP to destroy as a cost to send this transaction. See Transaction Cost for details.

attributes.name: flags: int | List[int] = 0
attributes.description: The Flags value of the EnableAmendment pseudo-transaction indicates the status of the amendment at the time of the ledger including the pseudo-transaction. A Flags value of 0 (no flags) or an omitted Flags field indicates that the amendment has been enabled, and applies to all ledgers afterward.

attributes.name: last_ledger_sequence: int | None = None
attributes.description: The highest ledger index this transaction can appear in. Specifying this field places a strict upper limit on how long the transaction can wait to be validated or rejected. See Reliable Transaction Submission for details.

attributes.name: ledger_sequence: int
attributes.description: The ledger index where this pseudo-transaction appears. This distinguishes the pseudo-transaction from other occurrences of the same change. This field is required.

attributes.name: memos: List[Memo] | None = None
attributes.description: Additional arbitrary information attached to this transaction.

attributes.name: network_id: int | None = None
attributes.description: The network id of the transaction.

attributes.name: sequence: int = 0
attributes.description: (Auto-fillable) The sequence number of the transaction. Must match the sending account’s next unused sequence number. See Account Sequence for details.

attributes.name: signers: List[Signer] | None = None
attributes.description: Signing data authorizing a multi-signed transaction. Added during multi-signing.

attributes.name: signing_pub_key: str = ''
attributes.description: The public key authorizing a single-signed transaction. Automatically added during signing.

attributes.name: source_tag: None = None
attributes.description: An arbitrary source tag representing a hosted user or specific purpose at the sending account where this transaction comes from.

attributes.name: tf_got_majority: bool | None = None
attributes.description: Support for this amendment increased to at least 80% of trusted validators starting with this ledger version.

attributes.name: tf_lost_majority: bool | None = None
attributes.description: Support for this amendment decreased to less than 80% of trusted validators starting with this ledger version.

attributes.name: ticket_sequence: int | None = None
attributes.description: The sequence number of the ticket to use in place of a Sequence number. If this is provided, sequence must be 0. Cannot be used with account_txn_id.

attributes.name: txn_signature: str = ''
attributes.description: The cryptographic signature from the sender that authorizes this transaction. Automatically added during signing.

classes.name: EnableAmendmentFlag
classes.signature: class xrpl.models.transactions.pseudo_transactions.EnableAmendmentFlag(value, names=None, *, module=None, qualname=None, type=None, start=1, boundary=None)
classes.description:
Bases: int, Enum
The Flags value of the EnableAmendment pseudo-transaction indicates the status of the amendment at the time of the ledger including the pseudo-transaction.
A Flags value of 0 (no flags) or an omitted Flags field indicates that the amendment has been enabled, and applies to all ledgers afterward.
See EnableAmendment Flags
Support for this amendment increased to at least 80% of trusted validators starting with this ledger version.
Support for this amendment decreased to less than 80% of trusted validators starting with this ledger version.
Return integer ratio.
Return a pair of integers, whose ratio is exactly equal to the original int and with a positive denominator.
Number of ones in the binary representation of the absolute value of self.
Also known as the population count.
Number of bits necessary to represent self in binary.
Returns self, the complex conjugate of any int.
the denominator of a rational number in lowest terms
Return the integer represented by the given array of bytes.
Holds the array of bytes to convert. The argument must either support the buffer protocol or be an iterable object producing bytes. Bytes and bytearray are examples of built-in objects that support the buffer protocol.
The byte order used to represent the integer. If byteorder is ‘big’, the most significant byte is at the beginning of the byte array. If byteorder is ‘little’, the most significant byte is at the end of the byte array. To request the native byte order of the host system, use `sys.byteorder’ as the byte order value. Default is to use ‘big’.
Indicates whether two’s complement is used to represent the integer.
the imaginary part of a complex number
the numerator of a rational number in lowest terms
the real part of a complex number
Return an array of bytes representing an integer.
Length of bytes object to use. An OverflowError is raised if the integer is not representable with the given number of bytes. Default is length 1.
The byte order used to represent the integer. If byteorder is ‘big’, the most significant byte is at the beginning of the byte array. If byteorder is ‘little’, the most significant byte is at the end of the byte array. To request the native byte order of the host system, use `sys.byteorder’ as the byte order value. Default is to use ‘big’.
Determines whether two’s complement is used to represent the integer. If signed is False and a negative integer is given, an OverflowError is raised.

methods.description:
Return integer ratio.
Return a pair of integers, whose ratio is exactly equal to the original int and with a positive denominator.

methods.description:
Number of ones in the binary representation of the absolute value of self.
Also known as the population count.

Return the integer represented by the given array of bytes.
Holds the array of bytes to convert. The argument must either support the buffer protocol or be an iterable object producing bytes. Bytes and bytearray are examples of built-in objects that support the buffer protocol.
The byte order used to represent the integer. If byteorder is ‘big’, the most significant byte is at the beginning of the byte array. If byteorder is ‘little’, the most significant byte is at the end of the byte array. To request the native byte order of the host system, use `sys.byteorder’ as the byte order value. Default is to use ‘big’.
Indicates whether two’s complement is used to represent the integer.

methods.name: to_bytes
methods.description:
Return an array of bytes representing an integer.
Length of bytes object to use. An OverflowError is raised if the integer is not representable with the given number of bytes. Default is length 1.
The byte order used to represent the integer. If byteorder is ‘big’, the most significant byte is at the beginning of the byte array. If byteorder is ‘little’, the most significant byte is at the end of the byte array. To request the native byte order of the host system, use `sys.byteorder’ as the byte order value. Default is to use ‘big’.
Determines whether two’s complement is used to represent the integer. If signed is False and a negative integer is given, an OverflowError is raised.

If E is present and has a .keys() method, then does: for k in E: D[k] = E[k] If E is present and lacks a .keys() method, then does: for k, v in E: D[k] = v In either case, this is followed by: for k in F: D[k] = F[k]

methods.description: If E is present and has a .keys() method, then does: for k in E: D[k] = E[k] If E is present and lacks a .keys() method, then does: for k, v in E: D[k] = v In either case, this is followed by: for k in F: D[k] = F[k]

classes.name: SetFee
classes.signature: class xrpl.models.transactions.pseudo_transactions.SetFee(*, account_txn_id: str | None = None, flags: Dict[str, bool] | int | List[int] = 0, last_ledger_sequence: int | None = None, memos: List[Memo] | None = None, signers: List[Signer] | None = None, ticket_sequence: int | None = None, network_id: int | None = None, base_fee: str | None = None, reference_fee_units: int | None = None, reserve_base: int | None = None, reserve_increment: int | None = None, base_fee_drops: str | None = None, reserve_base_drops: str | None = None, reserve_increment_drops: str | None = None)
classes.description:
Bases: PseudoTransaction
A SetFee pseudo-transaction marks a change in transaction cost or reserve requirements as a result of Fee Voting.
The parameters are different depending on if this is before or after the `XRPFees Amendment<https://xrpl.org/known-amendments.html#xrpfees>`_
Before the XRPFees Amendment which was proposed in rippled 1.10.0 base_fee, reference_fee_units, reserve_base, and reserve_increment were required fields.
After the XRPFees Amendment, base_fee_drops, reserve_base_drops, and reserve_increment_drops are required fields.
No SetFee Pseudo Transaction should contain fields from BOTH before and after the XRPFees amendment.
The address of the sender of the transaction. Required.
A hash value identifying a previous transaction from the same sender. If provided, this transaction is only considered valid if the identified transaction is the most recent transaction sent by this address. See AccountTxnID for details.
The charge, in drops of XRP, for the reference transaction, as hex. (This is the transaction cost before scaling for load.) This field is required.
The charge, in drops of XRP, for the reference transaction, as hex. (This is the transaction cost before scaling for load.) This field is required.
Creates the canonical binary format of the Transaction object.
The binary-encoded object, as a hexadecimal string.
(Auto-fillable) The amount of XRP to destroy as a cost to send this transaction. See Transaction Cost for details.
A List of flags, or a bitwise map of flags, modifying this transaction’s behavior. See Flags Field for more details.

Note: This only checks the exact model, and does not count model inheritance. This method returns False if the dictionary represents a subclass of this class.
dictionary – The dictionary to check. Note: The input dictionary can be of non-dict type. For instance, a str representation of JSON.
True if dictionary is a dict representation of an instance of this class; False if not.
Checks if a transaction has been signed.
Whether the transaction has been signed
Returns whether this BaseModel is valid.
Whether this BaseModel is valid.
The highest ledger index this transaction can appear in. Specifying this field places a strict upper limit on how long the transaction can wait to be validated or rejected. See Reliable Transaction Submission for details.
Additional arbitrary information attached to this transaction.
The network id of the transaction.
The cost, in fee units, of the reference transaction. This field is required.
The base reserve, in drops. This field is required.
The base reserve, in drops. This field is required.
The incremental reserve, in drops. This field is required.
The incremental reserve, in drops. This field is required.
(Auto-fillable) The sequence number of the transaction. Must match the sending account’s next unused sequence number. See Account Sequence for details.
Signing data authorizing a multi-signed transaction. Added during multi-signing.
The public key authorizing a single-signed transaction. Automatically added during signing.
An arbitrary source tag representing a hosted user or specific purpose at the sending account where this transaction comes from.
The sequence number of the ticket to use in place of a Sequence number. If this is provided, sequence must be 0. Cannot be used with account_txn_id.
Returns the dictionary representation of a Transaction.
The dictionary representation of a Transaction.
Creates a JSON-like dictionary in the JSON format used by the binary codec based on the Transaction object.
A JSON-like dictionary in the JSON format used by the binary codec.
The cryptographic signature from the sender that authorizes this transaction. Automatically added during signing.
Raises if this object is invalid.

attributes.description: The highest ledger index this transaction can appear in. Specifying this field places a strict upper limit on how long the transaction can wait to be validated or rejected. See Reliable Transaction Submission for details.

attributes.name: memos: List[Memo] | None = None
attributes.description: Additional arbitrary information attached to this transaction.

attributes.name: network_id: int | None = None
attributes.description: The network id of the transaction.

attributes.name: reference_fee_units: int | None
attributes.description: The cost, in fee units, of the reference transaction. This field is required.

attributes.name: reserve_base: int | None
attributes.description: The base reserve, in drops. This field is required.

attributes.name: reserve_base_drops: str | None
attributes.description: The base reserve, in drops. This field is required.

attributes.name: reserve_increment: int | None
attributes.description: The incremental reserve, in drops. This field is required.

attributes.name: reserve_increment_drops: str | None
attributes.description: The incremental reserve, in drops. This field is required.

attributes.name: sequence: int = 0
attributes.description: (Auto-fillable) The sequence number of the transaction. Must match the sending account’s next unused sequence number. See Account Sequence for details.

attributes.name: signers: List[Signer] | None = None
attributes.description: Signing data authorizing a multi-signed transaction. Added during multi-signing.

attributes.name: signing_pub_key: str = ''
attributes.description: The public key authorizing a single-signed transaction. Automatically added during signing.

attributes.name: source_tag: None = None
attributes.description: An arbitrary source tag representing a hosted user or specific purpose at the sending account where this transaction comes from.

attributes.name: ticket_sequence: int | None = None
attributes.description: The sequence number of the ticket to use in place of a Sequence number. If this is provided, sequence must be 0. Cannot be used with account_txn_id.

attributes.name: txn_signature: str = ''
attributes.description: The cryptographic signature from the sender that authorizes this transaction. Automatically added during signing.

classes.name: UNLModify
classes.signature: class xrpl.models.transactions.pseudo_transactions.UNLModify(*, account_txn_id: Optional[str] = None, flags: int = 0, last_ledger_sequence: Optional[int] = None, memos: Optional[List[Memo]] = None, signers: Optional[List[Signer]] = None, ticket_sequence: Optional[int] = None, network_id: Optional[int] = None, ledger_sequence: int = <object object>, unl_modify_disabling: int = <object object>, unl_modify_validator: str = <object object>)
classes.description:
Bases: PseudoTransaction
A UNLModify pseudo-transaction marks a change to the Negative UNL, indicating that a trusted validator has gone offline or come back online.
The address of the sender of the transaction. Required.
A hash value identifying a previous transaction from the same sender. If provided, this transaction is only considered valid if the identified transaction is the most recent transaction sent by this address. See AccountTxnID for details.

methods.name: EnableAmendment
methods.signature: class xrpl.models.transactions.pseudo_transactions.EnableAmendment(*, account_txn_id: Optional[str] = None, flags: int | ~typing.List[int] = 0, last_ledger_sequence: Optional[int] = None, memos: Optional[List[Memo]] = None, signers: Optional[List[Signer]] = None, ticket_sequence: Optional[int] = None, network_id: Optional[int] = None, amendment: str = <object object>, ledger_sequence: int = <object object>, tf_got_majority: bool | None = None, tf_lost_majority: bool | None = None)
methods.description:
Bases: PseudoTransaction
An EnableAmendment pseudo-transaction marks a change in status of an amendment to the XRP Ledger protocol, including:
A proposed amendment gained supermajority approval from validators.
A proposed amendment lost supermajority approval.
A proposed amendment has been enabled.
The address of the sender of the transaction. Required.
A hash value identifying a previous transaction from the same sender. If provided, this transaction is only considered valid if the identified transaction is the most recent transaction sent by this address. See AccountTxnID for details.
A unique identifier for the amendment. This is not intended to be a human-readable name. See Amendments for a list of known amendments. This field is required.
Creates the canonical binary format of the Transaction object.
The binary-encoded object, as a hexadecimal string.
(Auto-fillable) The amount of XRP to destroy as a cost to send this transaction. See Transaction Cost for details.
The Flags value of the EnableAmendment pseudo-transaction indicates the status of the amendment at the time of the ledger including the pseudo-transaction. A Flags value of 0 (no flags) or an omitted Flags field indicates that the amendment has been enabled, and applies to all ledgers afterward.
Decodes a transaction blob.
tx_blob – the tx blob to decode.
The formatted transaction.

True if dictionary is a dict representation of an instance of this class; False if not.

Whether the transaction has been signed
Returns whether this BaseModel is valid.
Whether this BaseModel is valid.
The highest ledger index this transaction can appear in. Specifying this field places a strict upper limit on how long the transaction can wait to be validated or rejected. See Reliable Transaction Submission for details.
The ledger index where this pseudo-transaction appears. This distinguishes the pseudo-transaction from other occurrences of the same change. This field is required.
Additional arbitrary information attached to this transaction.
The network id of the transaction.
(Auto-fillable) The sequence number of the transaction. Must match the sending account’s next unused sequence number. See Account Sequence for details.
Signing data authorizing a multi-signed transaction. Added during multi-signing.
The public key authorizing a single-signed transaction. Automatically added during signing.
An arbitrary source tag representing a hosted user or specific purpose at the sending account where this transaction comes from.
Support for this amendment increased to at least 80% of trusted validators starting with this ledger version.
Support for this amendment decreased to less than 80% of trusted validators starting with this ledger version.
The sequence number of the ticket to use in place of a Sequence number. If this is provided, sequence must be 0. Cannot be used with account_txn_id.

The dictionary representation of a Transaction.

methods.name: EnableAmendmentFlag
methods.signature: class xrpl.models.transactions.pseudo_transactions.EnableAmendmentFlag(value, names=None, *, module=None, qualname=None, type=None, start=1, boundary=None)
methods.description:
Bases: int, Enum
The Flags value of the EnableAmendment pseudo-transaction indicates the status of the amendment at the time of the ledger including the pseudo-transaction.
A Flags value of 0 (no flags) or an omitted Flags field indicates that the amendment has been enabled, and applies to all ledgers afterward.
See EnableAmendment Flags
Support for this amendment increased to at least 80% of trusted validators starting with this ledger version.
Support for this amendment decreased to less than 80% of trusted validators starting with this ledger version.
Return integer ratio.
Return a pair of integers, whose ratio is exactly equal to the original int and with a positive denominator.
Number of ones in the binary representation of the absolute value of self.
Also known as the population count.
Number of bits necessary to represent self in binary.
Returns self, the complex conjugate of any int.
the denominator of a rational number in lowest terms
Return the integer represented by the given array of bytes.
Holds the array of bytes to convert. The argument must either support the buffer protocol or be an iterable object producing bytes. Bytes and bytearray are examples of built-in objects that support the buffer protocol.
The byte order used to represent the integer. If byteorder is ‘big’, the most significant byte is at the beginning of the byte array. If byteorder is ‘little’, the most significant byte is at the end of the byte array. To request the native byte order of the host system, use `sys.byteorder’ as the byte order value. Default is to use ‘big’.
Indicates whether two’s complement is used to represent the integer.
the imaginary part of a complex number
the numerator of a rational number in lowest terms
the real part of a complex number
Return an array of bytes representing an integer.
Length of bytes object to use. An OverflowError is raised if the integer is not representable with the given number of bytes. Default is length 1.
The byte order used to represent the integer. If byteorder is ‘big’, the most significant byte is at the beginning of the byte array. If byteorder is ‘little’, the most significant byte is at the end of the byte array. To request the native byte order of the host system, use `sys.byteorder’ as the byte order value. Default is to use ‘big’.
Determines whether two’s complement is used to represent the integer. If signed is False and a negative integer is given, an OverflowError is raised.

Pairs are returned in LIFO (last-in, first-out) order. Raises KeyError if the dict is empty.

If E is present and has a .keys() method, then does: for k in E: D[k] = E[k] If E is present and lacks a .keys() method, then does: for k, v in E: D[k] = v In either case, this is followed by: for k in F: D[k] = F[k]

methods.name: update
methods.signature: update([E, ]**F) → None.  Update D from dict/iterable E and F.
methods.description: If E is present and has a .keys() method, then does: for k in E: D[k] = E[k] If E is present and lacks a .keys() method, then does: for k, v in E: D[k] = v In either case, this is followed by: for k in F: D[k] = F[k]

methods.name: values
methods.signature: values() → an object providing a view on D's values

methods.name: SetFee
methods.signature: class xrpl.models.transactions.pseudo_transactions.SetFee(*, account_txn_id: str | None = None, flags: Dict[str, bool] | int | List[int] = 0, last_ledger_sequence: int | None = None, memos: List[Memo] | None = None, signers: List[Signer] | None = None, ticket_sequence: int | None = None, network_id: int | None = None, base_fee: str | None = None, reference_fee_units: int | None = None, reserve_base: int | None = None, reserve_increment: int | None = None, base_fee_drops: str | None = None, reserve_base_drops: str | None = None, reserve_increment_drops: str | None = None)
methods.description:
Bases: PseudoTransaction
A SetFee pseudo-transaction marks a change in transaction cost or reserve requirements as a result of Fee Voting.
The parameters are different depending on if this is before or after the `XRPFees Amendment<https://xrpl.org/known-amendments.html#xrpfees>`_
Before the XRPFees Amendment which was proposed in rippled 1.10.0 base_fee, reference_fee_units, reserve_base, and reserve_increment were required fields.
After the XRPFees Amendment, base_fee_drops, reserve_base_drops, and reserve_increment_drops are required fields.
No SetFee Pseudo Transaction should contain fields from BOTH before and after the XRPFees amendment.
The address of the sender of the transaction. Required.
A hash value identifying a previous transaction from the same sender. If provided, this transaction is only considered valid if the identified transaction is the most recent transaction sent by this address. See AccountTxnID for details.
The charge, in drops of XRP, for the reference transaction, as hex. (This is the transaction cost before scaling for load.) This field is required.
The charge, in drops of XRP, for the reference transaction, as hex. (This is the transaction cost before scaling for load.) This field is required.
Creates the canonical binary format of the Transaction object.
The binary-encoded object, as a hexadecimal string.
(Auto-fillable) The amount of XRP to destroy as a cost to send this transaction. See Transaction Cost for details.
A List of flags, or a bitwise map of flags, modifying this transaction’s behavior. See Flags Field for more details.
Decodes a transaction blob.
tx_blob – the tx blob to decode.
The formatted transaction.
Construct a new Transaction from a dictionary of parameters.
value – The value to construct the Transaction from.
A new Transaction object, constructed using the given parameters.
XRPLModelException – If the dictionary provided is invalid.
Creates a Transaction object based on a JSON or JSON-string representation of data
In Payment transactions, the DeliverMax field is renamed to the Amount field.
value – The dictionary or JSON string to be instantiated.
A Transaction object instantiated from the input.
XRPLModelException – If Payment transactions have different values for amount and deliver_max fields
Hashes the Transaction object as the ledger does. Only valid for signed Transaction objects.
The hash of the Transaction object.
XRPLModelException – if the Transaction is unsigned.
Returns the correct transaction type based on the string name.
transaction_type – The String name of the Transaction object.
The transaction class with the given name.
XRPLModelException – If transaction_type is not a valid Transaction type.
Returns whether the transaction has the given flag value set.
flag – The given flag value for which the function will determine whether it is set.
Whether the transaction has the given flag value set.
Checks whether the provided dictionary is a dictionary representation of this class.
Note: This only checks the exact model, and does not count model inheritance. This method returns False if the dictionary represents a subclass of this class.
dictionary – The dictionary to check. Note: The input dictionary can be of non-dict type. For instance, a str representation of JSON.
True if dictionary is a dict representation of an instance of this class; False if not.
Checks if a transaction has been signed.
Whether the transaction has been signed
Returns whether this BaseModel is valid.
Whether this BaseModel is valid.
The highest ledger index this transaction can appear in. Specifying this field places a strict upper limit on how long the transaction can wait to be validated or rejected. See Reliable Transaction Submission for details.
Additional arbitrary information attached to this transaction.
The network id of the transaction.
The cost, in fee units, of the reference transaction. This field is required.
The base reserve, in drops. This field is required.
The base reserve, in drops. This field is required.
The incremental reserve, in drops. This field is required.
The incremental reserve, in drops. This field is required.
(Auto-fillable) The sequence number of the transaction. Must match the sending account’s next unused sequence number. See Account Sequence for details.
Signing data authorizing a multi-signed transaction. Added during multi-signing.
The public key authorizing a single-signed transaction. Automatically added during signing.
An arbitrary source tag representing a hosted user or specific purpose at the sending account where this transaction comes from.
The sequence number of the ticket to use in place of a Sequence number. If this is provided, sequence must be 0. Cannot be used with account_txn_id.
Returns the dictionary representation of a Transaction.
The dictionary representation of a Transaction.
Creates a JSON-like dictionary in the JSON format used by the binary codec based on the Transaction object.
A JSON-like dictionary in the JSON format used by the binary codec.
The cryptographic signature from the sender that authorizes this transaction. Automatically added during signing.
Raises if this object is invalid.
XRPLModelException – if this object is invalid.

dictionary – The dictionary to check. Note: The input dictionary can be of non-dict type. For instance, a str representation of JSON.
returns: The binary-encoded object, as a hexadecimal string.
The formatted transaction.

methods.name: UNLModify
methods.signature: class xrpl.models.transactions.pseudo_transactions.UNLModify(*, account_txn_id: Optional[str] = None, flags: int = 0, last_ledger_sequence: Optional[int] = None, memos: Optional[List[Memo]] = None, signers: Optional[List[Signer]] = None, ticket_sequence: Optional[int] = None, network_id: Optional[int] = None, ledger_sequence: int = <object object>, unl_modify_disabling: int = <object object>, unl_modify_validator: str = <object object>)
methods.description:
Bases: PseudoTransaction
A UNLModify pseudo-transaction marks a change to the Negative UNL, indicating that a trusted validator has gone offline or come back online.
The address of the sender of the transaction. Required.
A hash value identifying a previous transaction from the same sender. If provided, this transaction is only considered valid if the identified transaction is the most recent transaction sent by this address. See AccountTxnID for details.
Creates the canonical binary format of the Transaction object.
The binary-encoded object, as a hexadecimal string.
(Auto-fillable) The amount of XRP to destroy as a cost to send this transaction. See Transaction Cost for details.
The Flags value of the EnableAmendment pseudo-transaction indicates the status of the amendment at the time of the ledger including the pseudo-transaction. A Flags value of 0 (no flags) or an omitted Flags field indicates that the amendment has been enabled, and applies to all ledgers afterward.
Decodes a transaction blob.
tx_blob – the tx blob to decode.
The formatted transaction.
Construct a new Transaction from a dictionary of parameters.
value – The value to construct the Transaction from.
A new Transaction object, constructed using the given parameters.
XRPLModelException – If the dictionary provided is invalid.

Whether the transaction has been signed

Whether this BaseModel is valid.
The highest ledger index this transaction can appear in. Specifying this field places a strict upper limit on how long the transaction can wait to be validated or rejected. See Reliable Transaction Submission for details.
The ledger index where this pseudo-transaction appears. This distinguishes the pseudo-transaction from other occurrences of the same change. This field is required.
Additional arbitrary information attached to this transaction.
The network id of the transaction.
(Auto-fillable) The sequence number of the transaction. Must match the sending account’s next unused sequence number. See Account Sequence for details.

A JSON-like dictionary in the JSON format used by the binary codec.
The cryptographic signature from the sender that authorizes this transaction. Automatically added during signing.
If 1, this change represents adding a validator to the Negative UNL. If 0, this change represents removing a validator from the Negative UNL. (No other values are allowed.) This field is required.
The validator to add or remove, as identified by its master public key. This field is required.
Raises if this object is invalid.
XRPLModelException – if this object is invalid.
